prose:
Frontier-orbital energies of pyridine
Property: Kohn–Sham HOMO and LUMO energies (gas-phase), plus HOMO–LUMO gap
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RIJK density fitting (PySCF backend)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`); single-point calculation at that fixed geometry (no re-optimization)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model)
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF (invoked via chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill)
SCF convergence: converged in 10 cycles; no warnings reported
Backend integrity verdict: trustworthy = true

Results (all energies in eV):
• HOMO (index 20) ............. −7.2525992
• LUMO (index 21) ............. −1.112786
• HOMO–LUMO gap ............... 6.1398131

(The gap is a Koopmans-type orbital energy difference; it is **not** an optical excitation energy.)

Calculation launched with:
`chemkit frontier-orbitals --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`
A live engine log was written during execution (path provided at run start). No engine warnings were issued.
